    72→
    73→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→# macOS: bootstrap Colima + Incus (no-op on Linux)
    75→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    76→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    77→source "$SCRIPT_DIR/incus.macos.setup"
    78→
    79→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80→# Dependency checks
    81→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    82→require_commands() {
    83→  local missing=()
    84→  for cmd in "$@"; do
    85→    command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1 || missing+=("$cmd")
    86→  done
    87→  if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    88→    error "Missing required commands: ${missing[*]}"
    89→  fi
    90→}
    91→
    92→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    93→# Container readiness polling (replaces fragile sleep)
    94→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95→wait_for_container() {
    96→  local name="$1" timeout="${2:-30}" elapsed=0
    97→  local ready=0
    98→  while [[ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]]; do
    99→    if incus exec "$name" -- true >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   100→      ready=1
   101→      break
   102→    fi
   103→    sleep 1
   104→    elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
   105→  done
   106→  if [[ $ready -eq 0 ]]; then
   107→    error "Container $name not ready after ${timeout}s"
   108→  fi
   109→  return 0
   110→}
   111→
   112→# Wait for networking inside the container (can lag behind boot/restart)
   113→wait_for_network() {
   114→  local name="$1" timeout="${2:-30}" elapsed=0
   115→  log "Waiting for container networking..."
   116→  local ready=0
   117→  while [[ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]]; do
   118→    if incus exec "$name" -- sh -c "getent hosts github.com >/dev/null 2>&1"; then
   119→      ready=1
   120→      break
   121→    fi
   122→    sleep 2
   123→    elapsed=$((elapsed + 2))
   124→  done
   125→  if [[ $ready -eq 0 ]]; then
   126→    error "Container $name has no network after ${timeout}s"
   127→  fi
   128→  return 0
   129→}
   130→
   131→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   132→# subuid / subgid helpers (internal plumbing for raw.idmap fallback)
   133→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   134→id_in_subid_file() {
   135→  local id=$1 file=$2
   136→  [[ ! -f "$file" ]] && return 1
   137→  while IFS=: read -r name start count || [[ -n "$name" ]]; do
   138→    [[ -z "$name" || "$name" == \#* || "$name" != "root" ]] && continue
   139→    local end=$((start + count - 1))
   140→    [[ $id -ge $start && $id -le $end ]] && return 0
   141→  done < "$file"
   142→  return 1
   143→}
   144→
   145→entry_exists_in_file() {
   146→  local entry=$1 file=$2
   147→  [[ ! -f "$file" ]] && return 1
   148→  grep -qxF "$entry" "$file" 2>/dev/null
   149→}
   150→
   151→ensure_subid_entries() {
   152→  local uid="$1" gid="$2"
   153→  local subuid_ok=0 subgid_ok=0
   154→
   155→  id_in_subid_file "$uid" /etc/subuid && subuid_ok=1
   156→  id_in_subid_file "$gid" /etc/subgid && subgid_ok=1
   157→
   158→  [[ "$subuid_ok" == "1" && "$subgid_ok" == "1" ]] && return 0
   159→
   160→  echo ""
   161→  echo "Your UID/GID must be in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for write access."
   162→  local entries=()
   163→  [[ "$subuid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$uid:1" /etc/subuid && \
   164→    entries+=("/etc/subuid: root:$uid:1")
   165→  [[ "$subgid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$gid:1" /etc/subgid && \
   166→    entries+=("/etc/subgid: root:$gid:1")
   167→
   168→  [[ ${#entries[@]} -eq 0 ]] && return 0
   169→
   170→  echo "Need to add (requires sudo):"
   171→  printf '  %s\n' "${entries[@]}"
   172→  read -rp "Add these entries? [Y/n] " reply
   173→  [[ "$reply" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]] && return 1
   174→
   175→  [[ "$subuid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$uid:1" /etc/subuid && \
   176→    echo "root:$uid:1" | sudo tee -a /etc/subuid > /dev/null
   177→  [[ "$subgid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$gid:1" /etc/subgid && \
   178→    echo "root:$gid:1" | sudo tee -a /etc/subgid > /dev/null
   179→}
   180→
   181→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   182→# Mount workspace with user mapping
   183→#
   184→# Tries shift=true first (clean, kernel-level, no config needed).
   185→# Falls back to raw.idmap (broader compat, needs subuid/subgid).
   186→# If both fail, mounts without mapping (read-only for container user).
   187→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   188→mount_workspace() {
   189→  local container="$1" source="$2" target="$3" uid="$4" gid="$5"
   190→
   191→  # Attempt 1: shift=true (kernel idmapped mounts, 5.12+)
   192→  log "Trying shift=true mount..."
   193→  if incus config device add "$container" workspace disk \
   194→      source="$source" path="$target" shift=true 2>/dev/null; then
   195→    log "Mounted with shift=true"
   196→    return 0
   197→  fi
   198→
   199→  # shift failed, clean up
   200→  incus config device remove "$container" workspace 2>/dev/null || true
   201→
   202→  # Attempt 2: raw.idmap
   203→  log "shift=true not supported, trying raw.idmap..."
   204→  if ensure_subid_entries "$uid" "$gid"; then
   205→    incus config device add "$container" workspace disk \
   206→      source="$source" path="$target"
   207→    incus config set "$container" raw.idmap "both $uid $uid"
   208→    log "Restarting container for raw.idmap..."
   209→    incus restart "$container"
   210→    wait_for_container "$container"
   211→    wait_for_network "$container"
   212→    log "Mounted with raw.idmap"
   213→    IDMAP_METHOD="raw"
   214→    return 0
   215→  fi
   216→
   217→  # Attempt 3: no mapping
   218→  warn "Could not configure user mapping. Mounting without write access."
   219→  incus config device add "$container" workspace disk \
   220→    source="$source" path="$target"
   221→  IDMAP_METHOD="none"
   222→  return 0
   223→}
   224→
   225→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   226→# Cleanup trap — offer to delete container on failure
   227→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   228→cleanup_on_failure() {
   229→  if [[ -n "${CONTAINER_NAME:-}" ]] && incus info "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   230→    echo ""
   231→    warn "Script failed. Container '$CONTAINER_NAME' was partially created."
   232→    read -rp "Delete it? [Y/n] " reply
   233→    if [[ ! "$reply" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]]; then
   234→      incus delete --force "$CONTAINER_NAME" 2>/dev/null || true
   235→      log "Deleted $CONTAINER_NAME"
   236→    else
   237→      warn "Container left in place. Clean up manually with: incus delete --force $CONTAINER_NAME"
   238→    fi
   239→  fi
   240→}
   241→
   242→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   243→# Defaults
   244→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   245→WORKSPACE_PATH="$PWD"
   246→MOUNT_PATH="/workspace"
   247→BASE_IMAGE="images:alpine/3.23"
   248→CONTAINER_NAME=""
   249→USE_PROXY=0
   250→PROXY_PORT=""
   251→PROXY_IP=""
   252→USE_1PASSWORD=0
   253→ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY=""
   254→USE_GH_TOKEN=0
   255→GH_TOKEN_VALUE=""
   256→GH_USER_NAME=""
   257→GH_USER_EMAIL=""
   258→USE_ENTIRE=0
   259→DRY_RUN=0
   260→MAP_USER=0
   261→IDMAP_METHOD=""
   262→
   263→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   264→# Parse arguments
   265→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   266→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
   267→  case "$1" in
   268→    -p|--path)       [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; WORKSPACE_PATH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   269→    -m|--mount-path) [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; MOUNT_PATH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   270→    -i|--image)      [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; BASE_IMAGE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   271→    --ubuntu)        BASE_IMAGE="images:ubuntu/24.04"; shift ;;
   272→    --proxy)         USE_PROXY=1; shift ;;
   273→    --proxy-port)    [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; PROXY_PORT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   274→    --proxy-ip)      [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; PROXY_IP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   275→    --1pass)         USE_1PASSWORD=1; shift ;;
   276→    --gh-token)      USE_GH_TOKEN=1; shift ;;
   277→    --entire)        USE_ENTIRE=1; shift ;;
   278→    --colima-cpus)   [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; COLIMA_CPUS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   279→    --colima-memory) [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; COLIMA_MEMORY="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   280→    --colima-disk)   [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; COLIMA_DISK="$2"; shift 2 ;;
   281→    --dry-run)       DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
   282→    -h|--help)       usage ;;
   283→    -*)              error "Unknown option: $1" ;;
   284→    *)               CONTAINER_NAME="$1"; shift ;;
   285→  esac
   286→done
   287→
   288→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   289→# macOS pre-flight (noop on Linux)
   290→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   291→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
   292→source "$SCRIPT_DIR/incus.macos.setup"
   293→
   294→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   295→# Validate
   296→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   297→require_commands incus curl sudo realpath
   298→
   299→[[ -z "$CONTAINER_NAME" ]] && { warn "Container name required"; echo ""; usage; }
   300→[[ ! "$CONTAINER_NAME" =~ ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9-]*$ ]] && error "Invalid container name: must start with a letter, only letters/digits/hyphens"
   301→incus info "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 && error "Container '$CONTAINER_NAME' already exists"
   302→[[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" && -z "$PROXY_PORT" ]] && error "Proxy enabled but no port provided"
   303→[[ -n "$PROXY_PORT" && ! "$PROXY_PORT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && error "Proxy port must be numeric"
   304→
   305→WORKSPACE_PATH="${WORKSPACE_PATH/#\~/$HOME}"
   306→WORKSPACE_PATH="$(realpath "$WORKSPACE_PATH")"
   307→[[ ! -d "$WORKSPACE_PATH" ]] && error "Directory not found: $WORKSPACE_PATH"
   308→
   309→HOST_USER="$(whoami)"
   310→HOST_UID="$(id -u)"
   311→HOST_GID="$(id -g)"
   312→
   313→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   314→# User mapping prompt — simple yes/no, implementation details are hidden
   315→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   316→echo ""
   317→read -rp "Map host user into container for workspace write access? [Y/n] " MAP_CHOICE
   318→if [[ ! "${MAP_CHOICE:-Y}" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]]; then
   319→  MAP_USER=1
   320→fi
   321→
   322→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   323→# 1Password token prompt
   324→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   325→if [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]]; then
   326→  echo ""
   327→  log "1Password CLI requires a service account token"
   328→  echo "Create one at: https://start.1password.com/settings/automation"
   329→  read -rsp "Enter token: " ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY
   330→  echo ""
   331→  [[ -z "$ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY" ]] && error "Token required"
   332→fi
   333→
   334→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   335→# GitHub token prompt
   336→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   337→if [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]]; then
   338→  echo ""
   339→  log "GitHub auth requires a fine-grained personal access token"
   340→  echo "Create one at: https://github.com/settings/tokens?type=beta"
   341→  echo "Recommended scopes: Contents (read/write), Metadata (read)"
   342→  read -rsp "Enter GitHub token: " GH_TOKEN_VALUE
   343→  echo ""
   344→  [[ -z "$GH_TOKEN_VALUE" ]] && error "GitHub token required"
   345→
   346→  DEFAULT_GH_NAME="$(git config --global user.name 2>/dev/null || true)"
   347→  DEFAULT_GH_EMAIL="$(git config --global user.email 2>/dev/null || true)"
   348→
   349→  read -rp "Git user.name [${DEFAULT_GH_NAME:-}]: " GH_USER_NAME
   350→  GH_USER_NAME="${GH_USER_NAME:-$DEFAULT_GH_NAME}"
   351→  [[ -z "$GH_USER_NAME" ]] && error "Git user.name required"
   352→
   353→  read -rp "Git user.email [${DEFAULT_GH_EMAIL:-}]: " GH_USER_EMAIL
   354→  GH_USER_EMAIL="${GH_USER_EMAIL:-$DEFAULT_GH_EMAIL}"
   355→  [[ -z "$GH_USER_EMAIL" ]] && error "Git user.email required"
   356→fi
   357→
   358→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   359→# Dry-run summary
   360→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   361→if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "1" ]]; then
   362→  echo ""
   363→  echo "========================================"
   364→  echo "DRY RUN — nothing will be created"
   365→  echo "========================================"
   366→  echo "Container:   $CONTAINER_NAME"
   367→  echo "Image:       $BASE_IMAGE"
   368→  echo "User:        $HOST_USER (UID:$HOST_UID GID:$HOST_GID)"
   369→  echo "Workspace:   $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"
   370→  echo "Write access: $( [[ "$MAP_USER" == "1" ]] && echo "yes (auto-detect method)" || echo "no" )"
   371→  echo "Chromium:    yes"
   372→  echo "Dev tools:   Oh My Zsh, fzf, bat"
   373→  echo "Docker:      yes"
   374→  echo "Proxy:       $( [[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]] && echo "${PROXY_IP:-auto}:$PROXY_PORT" || echo no )"
   375→  echo "1Password:   $( [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]] && echo yes || echo no )"
   376→  echo "GitHub auth: $( [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]] && echo "yes ($GH_USER_NAME <$GH_USER_EMAIL>)" || echo no )"
   377→  echo "========================================"
   378→  echo ""
   379→  echo "Would run:"
   380→  echo "  incus launch $BASE_IMAGE $CONTAINER_NAME"
   381→  echo "  Enable container nesting for Docker"
   382→  echo "  Install packages ($(echo "$BASE_IMAGE" | grep -q alpine && echo apk || echo apt))"
   383→  echo "  Create user $HOST_USER inside container"
   384→  echo "  Mount $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"
   385→  [[ "$MAP_USER" == "1" ]] && echo "  Map UID/GID (try shift, fallback to raw.idmap)"
   386→  echo "  Install: mise, Claude Code"
   387→  echo "  Install Chromium (headless)"
   388→  echo "  Install: Oh My Zsh, fzf, bat, shell aliases"
   389→  echo "  Install Docker"
   390→  [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]] && echo "  Install 1Password CLI (token via incus config env var)"
   391→  [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]] && echo "  Install gh CLI, configure git auth (token via incus config env var)"
   392→  [[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]] && echo "  Configure proxy"
   393→  exit 0
   394→fi
   395→
   396→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   397→# From here on, real work — set up cleanup trap
   398→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   399→trap cleanup_on_failure ERR
   400→
   401→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   402→# Create container
   403→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   404→log "Creating container: $CONTAINER_NAME ($(basename "$BASE_IMAGE"))"
   405→incus launch "$BASE_IMAGE" "$CONTAINER_NAME"
   406→
   407→log "Enabling container nesting for Docker..."
   408→incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" security.nesting=true
   409→incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" security.syscalls.intercept.mknod=true
   410→incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" security.syscalls.intercept.setxattr=true
   411→
   412→# Docker-in-Incus hits two AppArmor problems:
   413→#   1. runc fails with "ip_unprivileged_port_start: permission denied"
   414→#      unless the container runs with an unconfined AppArmor profile.
   415→#      (CVE-2025-52881, https://github.com/lxc/incus/pull/2624)
   416→#   2. Even with unconfined, Docker reads /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled,
   417→#      sees "Y", and tries to load the "docker-default" profile via securityfs —
   418→#      which isn't accessible inside the container.
   419→# Fix: set unconfined at the LXC level (fixes #1), then mask the kernel
   420→# AppArmor "enabled" flag inside the container so Docker skips AppArmor
   421→# entirely (fixes #2).  The Incus container itself remains the security
   422→# boundary.
   423→incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" raw.lxc "lxc.apparmor.profile=unconfined"
   424→log "AppArmor set to unconfined inside container (required for Docker)."
   425→# raw.lxc is only read at container start, so restart to apply it.
   426→incus restart "$CONTAINER_NAME"
   427→
   428→wait_for_container "$CONTAINER_NAME"
   429→wait_for_network "$CONTAINER_NAME"
   430→
   431→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   432→# Install packages
   433→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   434→log "Installing packages..."
   435→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -es <<PACKAGES
   436→  if command -v apk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   437→    # Ensure community repo is enabled (some Incus images only ship main)
   438→    if ! grep -q '/community' /etc/apk/repositories 2>/dev/null; then
   439→      MAIN_REPO=\$(grep '/main' /etc/apk/repositories | head -1)
   440→      echo "\${MAIN_REPO%/main}/community" >> /etc/apk/repositories
   441→    fi
   442→    apk update && apk add --no-cache $APK_CORE $APK_BUILD $APK_DEVLIBS $APK_RUNTIME
   443→  elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   444→    export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
   445→    apt-get update -y
   446→    apt-get install -y $APT_CORE $APT_BUILD $APT_DEVLIBS $APT_RUNTIME
   447→    apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
   448→
   449→
   450→    # Extra repos (mise + GitHub CLI)
   451→    install -dm 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
   452→    curl -fsSL https://mise.jdx.dev/gpg-key.pub | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/mise-archive-keyring.gpg
   453→    echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/mise-archive-keyring.gpg] https://mise.jdx.dev/deb stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mise.list
   454→    curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
   455→      | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg 2>/dev/null
   456→    chmod go+r /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
   457→    echo "deb [arch=\$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
   458→      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list
   459→    apt-get update -y
   460→    apt-get install -y mise gh
   461→
   462→  else
   463→    echo 'Unsupported package manager' >&2; exit 1
   464→  fi
   465→PACKAGES
   466→
   467→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   468→# Create user
   469→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   470→log "Creating user $HOST_USER..."
   471→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "
   472→  ZSH_PATH=\$(command -v zsh || echo /bin/bash)
   473→
   474→  # Remove conflicting UID/GID owners
   475→  EXISTING_USER=\$(getent passwd $HOST_UID | cut -d: -f1 || true)
   476→  EXISTING_GROUP=\$(getent group $HOST_GID | cut -d: -f1 || true)
   477→
   478→  if [ -n \"\$EXISTING_USER\" ] && [ \"\$EXISTING_USER\" != \"$HOST_USER\" ]; then
   479→    userdel -r \"\$EXISTING_USER\" 2>/dev/null || userdel \"\$EXISTING_USER\" 2>/dev/null || true
   480→  fi
   481→  if [ -n \"\$EXISTING_GROUP\" ] && [ \"\$EXISTING_GROUP\" != \"$HOST_USER\" ]; then
   482→    groupdel \"\$EXISTING_GROUP\" 2>/dev/null || true
   483→  fi
   484→
   485→  # Create group + user
   486→  if command -v useradd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   487→    groupadd -g $HOST_GID $HOST_USER 2>/dev/null || true
   488→    useradd -m -u $HOST_UID -g $HOST_GID -s \"\$ZSH_PATH\" $HOST_USER || exit 1
   489→  else
   490→    addgroup -g $HOST_GID $HOST_USER 2>/dev/null || true
   491→    adduser -D -u $HOST_UID -G $HOST_USER -s \"\$ZSH_PATH\" $HOST_USER || exit 1
   492→  fi
   493→
   494→  echo '$HOST_USER ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL' > /etc/sudoers.d/$HOST_USER
   495→  chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/$HOST_USER
   496→
   497→  id $HOST_USER >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'User creation failed' >&2; exit 1; }
   498→"
   499→
   500→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   501→# Mount workspace
   502→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   503→log "Mounting workspace: $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"
   504→
   505→if [[ "$MAP_USER" == "1" ]]; then
   506→  mount_workspace "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$WORKSPACE_PATH" "$MOUNT_PATH" "$HOST_UID" "$HOST_GID"
   507→else
   508→  incus config device add "$CONTAINER_NAME" workspace disk \
   509→    source="$WORKSPACE_PATH" path="$MOUNT_PATH"
   510→  IDMAP_METHOD="none"
   511→fi
   512→
   513→# Final readiness check
   514→wait_for_container "$CONTAINER_NAME"
   515→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- id "$HOST_USER" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
   516→  || error "User $HOST_USER does not exist. User creation may have failed."
   517→
   518→wait_for_network "$CONTAINER_NAME"
   519→
   520→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   521→# Shell setup — auto-cd and dev tools
   522→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   523→log "Configuring zsh..."
   524→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'ZSHEOF'
   525→  set -e
   526→
   527→  # Auto-cd to /workspace on login
   528→  touch ~/.zshrc
   529→  cat >> ~/.zprofile <<'WORKSPACE'
   530→if [[ -d /workspace ]]; then
   531→    cd /workspace
   532→fi
   533→WORKSPACE
   534→
   535→  # Activate mise in zsh
   536→  echo 'eval "$(mise activate zsh)"' >> ~/.zshrc
   537→ZSHEOF
   538→
   539→log "Installing Oh My Zsh..."
   540→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'ZSHEOF'
   541→  set -e
   542→  sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)" "" --unattended
   543→
   544→  sed -i 's/^ZSH_THEME=".*"/ZSH_THEME="dpoggi"/' ~/.zshrc
   545→
   546→  # Add [incus] prefix to prompt
   547→  cat >> ~/.zshrc <<'RCBLOCK'
   548→
   549→# [incus] prompt prefix
   550→PROMPT="%{$fg[cyan]%}[incus]%{$reset_color%} ${PROMPT%»*}%{$reset_color%}"$'\n'"» "
   551→RCBLOCK
   552→
   553→  # Plugins
   554→  git clone https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions \
   555→    ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-~/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions
   556→  sed -i 's/^plugins=(git)/plugins=(git zsh-autosuggestions)/' ~/.zshrc
   557→ZSHEOF
   558→
   559→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   560→# Docker
   561→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   562→log "Installing Docker..."
   563→
   564→# Mask AppArmor so Docker doesn't try to use it (see AppArmor comment above).
   565→# Bind-mount a file containing "N" over the kernel's AppArmor enabled flag.
   566→# Docker's Go apparmor library reads this to decide whether to use AppArmor;
   567→# returning "N" makes it skip AppArmor entirely.
   568→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c 'bash -s' <<'APPARMOR_EOF'
   569→    if [ -f /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled ] && \
   570→       grep -q Y /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled 2>/dev/null; then
   571→      echo N > /run/apparmor_disabled
   572→      mount --bind /run/apparmor_disabled /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled
   573→
   574→      # Make the mask persistent across reboots.
   575→      if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   576→        cat > /etc/systemd/system/mask-apparmor.service <<'UNIT'
   577→[Unit]
   578→Description=Mask AppArmor enabled flag for Docker-in-Incus
   579→DefaultDependencies=no
   580→Before=docker.service containerd.service docker.socket
   581→
   582→[Service]
   583→Type=oneshot
   584→RemainAfterExit=yes
   585→ExecStart=/bin/sh -c 'echo N > /run/apparmor_disabled && mount --bind /run/apparmor_disabled /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled'
   586→
   587→[Install]
   588→WantedBy=multi-user.target
   589→UNIT
   590→        systemctl daemon-reload
   591→        systemctl enable mask-apparmor.service
   592→      elif command -v rc-update >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   593→        # Alpine/OpenRC: create a proper init script with dependency ordering
   594→        cat > /etc/init.d/mask-apparmor <<'OPENRC'
   595→#!/sbin/openrc-run
   596→description="Mask AppArmor enabled flag for Docker-in-Incus"
   597→
   598→depend() {
   599→    before docker containerd
   600→    keyword -stop
   601→}
   602→
   603→start() {
   604→    echo N > /run/apparmor_disabled
   605→    mount --bind /run/apparmor_disabled /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled
   606→}
   607→OPENRC
   608→        chmod +x /etc/init.d/mask-apparmor
   609→        rc-update add mask-apparmor boot
   610→      fi
   611→    fi
   612→APPARMOR_EOF
   613→
   614→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c 'bash -s' <<'DOCKER_EOF'
   615→    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   616→      export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
   617→      apt-get update && apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupg
   618→      install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
   619→      curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
   620→      chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
   621→      echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list
   622→      apt-get update && apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin
   623→      systemctl enable docker && systemctl start docker
   624→    else
   625→      apk add --no-cache docker docker-cli-compose
   626→      rc-update add docker boot && service docker start
   627→    fi
   628→DOCKER_EOF
   629→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "usermod -aG docker $HOST_USER 2>/dev/null || addgroup $HOST_USER docker 2>/dev/null || true"
   630→wait_for_container "$CONTAINER_NAME"
   631→
   632→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   633→# Chromium
   634→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   635→log "Installing Playwright + Chromium dependencies..."
   636→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c 'bash -s' <<'CHROMIUM_EOF'
   637→    if command -v apk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   638→      apk add --no-cache nss freetype harfbuzz ca-certificates ttf-freefont \
   639→        mesa-gbm libxcomposite libxdamage libxrandr libxshmfence at-spi2-core \
   640→        cups-libs libdrm pango alsa-lib dbus-libs
   641→    elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   642→      export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
   643→      # Playwright's bundled Chromium needs.
   644→      apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   645→        libnss3 libnspr4 libatk1.0-0t64 libatk-bridge2.0-0t64 \
   646→        libcups2t64 libdrm2 libxcomposite1 libxdamage1 libxfixes3 \
   647→        libxrandr2 libgbm1 libpango-1.0-0 libcairo2 libasound2t64 \
   648→        libatspi2.0-0t64 libxshmfence1 fonts-liberation xdg-utils
   649→    fi
   650→CHROMIUM_EOF
   651→
   652→log "Installing Playwright + Chromium browser..."
   653→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- npm install -g playwright
   654→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'npx -y playwright install chromium'
   655→
   656→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   657→# fzf + bat (interactive search)
   658→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   659→log "Installing fzf and bat via mise..."
   660→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'EOF'
   661→  set -e
   662→  export MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS="/workspace"
   663→  mise use -g go@latest fzf@latest bat@latest
   664→
   665→  cat >> ~/.zshrc <<'FZF'
   666→
   667→# fzf + bat aliases
   668→alias f="fzf --preview 'bat {-1} --color=always'"
   669→gdiff() {
   670→  local preview="git diff $@ --color=always -- {-1}"
   671→  git diff $@ --name-only | fzf -m --ansi --preview $preview
   672→}
   673→FZF
   674→EOF
   675→
   676→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   677→# Entire CLI (optional)
   678→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   679→if [[ "$USE_ENTIRE" == "1" ]]; then
   680→  log "Installing Entire CLI..."
   681→  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'EOF'
   682→    set -e
   683→    eval "$(mise activate bash)"
   684→    go install github.com/entireio/cli@latest
   685→    entire enable
   686→EOF
   687→fi
   688→
   689→# Run mise install if the workspace has a config
   690→if [[ -f "$WORKSPACE_PATH/mise.toml" || -f "$WORKSPACE_PATH/.mise.toml" || -f "$WORKSPACE_PATH/.tool-versions" ]]; then
   691→  log "Running mise install for workspace..."
   692→  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'EOF'
   693→    export MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS=/workspace;
   694→    export MISE_NODE_COMPILE=0;
   695→    cd $MOUNT_PATH && mise install
   696→EOF
   697→fi
   698→
   699→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   700→# 1Password CLI (optional)
   701→#
   702→# The service account token is stored as a container-level environment
   703→# variable via `incus config set`. This avoids writing it to disk or
   704→# shell history inside the container. It is visible to anyone who can
   705→# run `incus config show <container>` on the host.
   706→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   707→if [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]]; then
   708→  log "Installing 1Password CLI..."
   709→  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c '
   710→    wget -q https://cache.agilebits.com/dist/1P/op2/pkg/v2.30.0/op_linux_amd64_v2.30.0.zip -O /tmp/op.zip
   711→    unzip -q /tmp/op.zip -d /tmp/ && mv /tmp/op /usr/local/bin/ && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/op && rm /tmp/op.zip
   712→  '
   713→  log "Setting 1Password token via container environment..."
   714→  incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" environment.OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="$ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY"
   715→  unset ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY
   716→fi
   717→
   718→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   719→# GitHub auth (optional)
   720→#
   721→# Injects the token as a container env var and configures git's credential
   722→# helper via `gh auth setup-git`. gh CLI is already installed as a base
   723→# package. Token never written to disk; unset from host shell after injection.
   724→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   725→if [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]]; then
   726→  log "Setting GitHub token via container environment..."
   727→  incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" environment.GH_TOKEN="$GH_TOKEN_VALUE"
   728→
   729→  log "Configuring git credential helper and identity..."
   730→  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" --env "GH_TOKEN=$GH_TOKEN_VALUE" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c "
   731→    gh auth setup-git
   732→    git config --global user.name '$GH_USER_NAME'
   733→    git config --global user.email '$GH_USER_EMAIL'
   734→  "
   735→  unset GH_TOKEN_VALUE
   736→fi
   737→
   738→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   739→# Claude Code
   740→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   741→incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'CLAUDE'
   742→  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
   743→  echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
   744→CLAUDE
   745→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   746→# Proxy (optional)
   747→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   748→if [[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]]; then
   749→  [[ -z "$PROXY_IP" ]] && PROXY_IP="$(incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "ip route | awk '/default/ {print \$3}'")"
   750→  log "Configuring proxy: $PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT"
   751→  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "cat >/etc/profile.d/claude-proxy.sh <<PROXYEOF
   752→export HTTP_PROXY='http://$PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT'
   753→export HTTPS_PROXY='http://$PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT'
   754→export NO_PROXY='localhost,127.0.0.1'
   755→PROXYEOF
   756→chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/claude-proxy.sh"
   757→fi
   758→
   759→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   760→# Disable cleanup trap on success
   761→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   762→trap - ERR
   763→
   764→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   765→# Done
   766→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   767→log "Container ready!"
   768→echo ""
   769→echo "========================================"
   770→echo "Container:   $CONTAINER_NAME"
   771→echo "Image:       $BASE_IMAGE"
   772→echo "User:        $HOST_USER (UID:$HOST_UID GID:$HOST_GID)"
   773→echo "Workspace:   $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"
   774→echo "Write access: ${IDMAP_METHOD:-none}"
   775→echo "Chromium:    installed"
   776→echo "Dev tools:   Oh My Zsh, fzf, bat"
   777→echo "Docker:      installed"
   778→[[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]]     && echo "Proxy:       http://$PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT"
   779→[[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]] && echo "1Password:   installed (token in container env)"
   780→[[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]] && echo "GitHub auth: configured ($GH_USER_NAME <$GH_USER_EMAIL>)"
   781→echo "========================================"
   782→echo ""
   783→echo "Shell in:"
   784→echo "  incus exec $CONTAINER_NAME -- su - $HOST_USER"
   785→echo ""
   786→echo "Start Claude Code:"
   787→echo "  incus exec $CONTAINER_NAME -- su - $HOST_USER -c 'cd $MOUNT_PATH && claude'"
   788→

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# init-incus-claude
# Creates an Incus container with Claude Code and a mounted workspace.
#
# Default: Alpine (lightweight, fast, small attack surface)
# Optional: Ubuntu 24.04 via --ubuntu (required for ML/AI workloads needing glibc)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Colors
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logging helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log()  { echo -e "${GREEN}[+]${NC} $1"; }
warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[!]${NC} $1"; }
error(){ echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1" >&2; exit 1; }

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Usage
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
usage() {
  cat << EOF
Usage: $(basename "$0") [OPTIONS] <container-name>

Create an Incus container with Claude Code and mounted workspace.
Default image is Alpine. Use --ubuntu for ML/AI workloads.
Includes Docker, Chromium (Playwright), Oh My Zsh, fzf, and bat by default.

Options:
  -p, --path PATH           Host directory to mount (default: current directory)
  -m, --mount-path PATH     Container mount point (default: /workspace)
  -i, --image IMAGE         Base image override (ignores --ubuntu)
  --ubuntu                  Use Ubuntu 24.04 (required for glibc-dependent
                            workloads like XLA, CUDA, and most ML/AI libraries)
  --proxy                   Enable HTTP(S) proxy env vars
  --proxy-port PORT         REQUIRED when --proxy is set
  --proxy-ip IP             Optional proxy IP override
  --1pass                   Install 1Password CLI
  --gh-token                Configure GitHub auth (prompts for PAT, sets up gh CLI)
  --entire                  Install Entire CLI (https://github.com/entireio/cli)
  --colima-cpus N           Colima VM CPUs (default: 4, macOS only)
  --colima-memory N         Colima VM memory in GB (default: 8, macOS only)
  --colima-disk N           Colima VM disk in GB (default: 100, macOS only)
  --dry-run                 Show what would be done without doing it
  -h, --help                Show this help
EOF
  exit 1
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Packages — grouped by purpose for readability
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# --- Alpine (apk) ---
APK_CORE="vim neovim bash curl git wget sudo unzip tmux zsh shadow mise github-cli"
APK_BUILD="build-base pkgconf autoconf automake bison re2c cmake"
APK_DEVLIBS="openssl-dev ncurses-dev readline-dev zlib-dev bzip2-dev sqlite-dev libffi-dev xz-dev yaml-dev libxml2-dev libxslt-dev curl-dev oniguruma-dev libzip-dev libpng-dev jpeg-dev freetype-dev gd-dev lksctp-tools-dev unixodbc-dev tk-dev"
APK_RUNTIME="ca-certificates fontconfig freetype python3 python3-dev py3-pip nodejs npm"

# --- Ubuntu/Debian (apt) ---
APT_CORE="bash curl git wget sudo unzip tmux zsh"
APT_BUILD="build-essential pkg-config autoconf automake bison cmake"
APT_DEVLIBS="libssl-dev libncurses-dev libreadline-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev libsqlite3-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev libyaml-dev libxml2-dev libxslt1-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev libonig-dev libzip-dev libpng-dev libjpeg-dev libfreetype6-dev libgd-dev unixodbc-dev"
APT_RUNTIME="gpg ca-certificates psmisc fontconfig fzf bat python3 python3-dev python3-pip python3-venv nodejs npm"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# macOS: bootstrap Colima + Incus (no-op on Linux)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "$SCRIPT_DIR/incus.macos.setup"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Dependency checks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
require_commands() {
  local missing=()
  for cmd in "$@"; do
    command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1 || missing+=("$cmd")
  done
  if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    error "Missing required commands: ${missing[*]}"
  fi
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Container readiness polling (replaces fragile sleep)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
wait_for_container() {
  local name="$1" timeout="${2:-30}" elapsed=0
  local ready=0
  while [[ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]]; do
    if incus exec "$name" -- true >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      ready=1
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
    elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
  done
  if [[ $ready -eq 0 ]]; then
    error "Container $name not ready after ${timeout}s"
  fi
  return 0
}

# Wait for networking inside the container (can lag behind boot/restart)
wait_for_network() {
  local name="$1" timeout="${2:-30}" elapsed=0
  log "Waiting for container networking..."
  local ready=0
  while [[ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]]; do
    if incus exec "$name" -- sh -c "getent hosts github.com >/dev/null 2>&1"; then
      ready=1
      break
    fi
    sleep 2
    elapsed=$((elapsed + 2))
  done
  if [[ $ready -eq 0 ]]; then
    error "Container $name has no network after ${timeout}s"
  fi
  return 0
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# subuid / subgid helpers (internal plumbing for raw.idmap fallback)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
id_in_subid_file() {
  local id=$1 file=$2
  [[ ! -f "$file" ]] && return 1
  while IFS=: read -r name start count || [[ -n "$name" ]]; do
    [[ -z "$name" || "$name" == \#* || "$name" != "root" ]] && continue
    local end=$((start + count - 1))
    [[ $id -ge $start && $id -le $end ]] && return 0
  done < "$file"
  return 1
}

entry_exists_in_file() {
  local entry=$1 file=$2
  [[ ! -f "$file" ]] && return 1
  grep -qxF "$entry" "$file" 2>/dev/null
}

ensure_subid_entries() {
  local uid="$1" gid="$2"
  local subuid_ok=0 subgid_ok=0

  id_in_subid_file "$uid" /etc/subuid && subuid_ok=1
  id_in_subid_file "$gid" /etc/subgid && subgid_ok=1

  [[ "$subuid_ok" == "1" && "$subgid_ok" == "1" ]] && return 0

  echo ""
  echo "Your UID/GID must be in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for write access."
  local entries=()
  [[ "$subuid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$uid:1" /etc/subuid && \
    entries+=("/etc/subuid: root:$uid:1")
  [[ "$subgid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$gid:1" /etc/subgid && \
    entries+=("/etc/subgid: root:$gid:1")

  [[ ${#entries[@]} -eq 0 ]] && return 0

  echo "Need to add (requires sudo):"
  printf '  %s\n' "${entries[@]}"
  read -rp "Add these entries? [Y/n] " reply
  [[ "$reply" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]] && return 1

  [[ "$subuid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$uid:1" /etc/subuid && \
    echo "root:$uid:1" | sudo tee -a /etc/subuid > /dev/null
  [[ "$subgid_ok" == "0" ]] && ! entry_exists_in_file "root:$gid:1" /etc/subgid && \
    echo "root:$gid:1" | sudo tee -a /etc/subgid > /dev/null
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Mount workspace with user mapping
#
# Tries shift=true first (clean, kernel-level, no config needed).
# Falls back to raw.idmap (broader compat, needs subuid/subgid).
# If both fail, mounts without mapping (read-only for container user).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
mount_workspace() {
  local container="$1" source="$2" target="$3" uid="$4" gid="$5"

  # Attempt 1: shift=true (kernel idmapped mounts, 5.12+)
  log "Trying shift=true mount..."
  if incus config device add "$container" workspace disk \
      source="$source" path="$target" shift=true 2>/dev/null; then
    log "Mounted with shift=true"
    return 0
  fi

  # shift failed, clean up
  incus config device remove "$container" workspace 2>/dev/null || true

  # Attempt 2: raw.idmap
  log "shift=true not supported, trying raw.idmap..."
  if ensure_subid_entries "$uid" "$gid"; then
    incus config device add "$container" workspace disk \
      source="$source" path="$target"
    incus config set "$container" raw.idmap "both $uid $uid"
    log "Restarting container for raw.idmap..."
    incus restart "$container"
    wait_for_container "$container"
    wait_for_network "$container"
    log "Mounted with raw.idmap"
    IDMAP_METHOD="raw"
    return 0
  fi

  # Attempt 3: no mapping
  warn "Could not configure user mapping. Mounting without write access."
  incus config device add "$container" workspace disk \
    source="$source" path="$target"
  IDMAP_METHOD="none"
  return 0
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Cleanup trap — offer to delete container on failure
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
cleanup_on_failure() {
  if [[ -n "${CONTAINER_NAME:-}" ]] && incus info "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo ""
    warn "Script failed. Container '$CONTAINER_NAME' was partially created."
    read -rp "Delete it? [Y/n] " reply
    if [[ ! "$reply" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]]; then
      incus delete --force "$CONTAINER_NAME" 2>/dev/null || true
      log "Deleted $CONTAINER_NAME"
    else
      warn "Container left in place. Clean up manually with: incus delete --force $CONTAINER_NAME"
    fi
  fi
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Defaults
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
WORKSPACE_PATH="$PWD"
MOUNT_PATH="/workspace"
BASE_IMAGE="images:alpine/3.23"
CONTAINER_NAME=""
USE_PROXY=0
PROXY_PORT=""
PROXY_IP=""
USE_1PASSWORD=0
ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY=""
USE_GH_TOKEN=0
GH_TOKEN_VALUE=""
GH_USER_NAME=""
GH_USER_EMAIL=""
USE_ENTIRE=0
DRY_RUN=0
MAP_USER=0
IDMAP_METHOD=""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Parse arguments
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    -p|--path)       [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; WORKSPACE_PATH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    -m|--mount-path) [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; MOUNT_PATH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    -i|--image)      [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; BASE_IMAGE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --ubuntu)        BASE_IMAGE="images:ubuntu/24.04"; shift ;;
    --proxy)         USE_PROXY=1; shift ;;
    --proxy-port)    [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; PROXY_PORT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --proxy-ip)      [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; PROXY_IP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --1pass)         USE_1PASSWORD=1; shift ;;
    --gh-token)      USE_GH_TOKEN=1; shift ;;
    --entire)        USE_ENTIRE=1; shift ;;
    --colima-cpus)   [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; COLIMA_CPUS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --colima-memory) [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; COLIMA_MEMORY="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --colima-disk)   [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && error "$1 requires an argument"; COLIMA_DISK="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --dry-run)       DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
    -h|--help)       usage ;;
    -*)              error "Unknown option: $1" ;;
    *)               CONTAINER_NAME="$1"; shift ;;
  esac
done

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# macOS pre-flight (noop on Linux)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "$SCRIPT_DIR/incus.macos.setup"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validate
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
require_commands incus curl sudo realpath

[[ -z "$CONTAINER_NAME" ]] && { warn "Container name required"; echo ""; usage; }
[[ ! "$CONTAINER_NAME" =~ ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9-]*$ ]] && error "Invalid container name: must start with a letter, only letters/digits/hyphens"
incus info "$CONTAINER_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 && error "Container '$CONTAINER_NAME' already exists"
[[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" && -z "$PROXY_PORT" ]] && error "Proxy enabled but no port provided"
[[ -n "$PROXY_PORT" && ! "$PROXY_PORT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && error "Proxy port must be numeric"

WORKSPACE_PATH="${WORKSPACE_PATH/#\~/$HOME}"
WORKSPACE_PATH="$(realpath "$WORKSPACE_PATH")"
[[ ! -d "$WORKSPACE_PATH" ]] && error "Directory not found: $WORKSPACE_PATH"

HOST_USER="$(whoami)"
HOST_UID="$(id -u)"
HOST_GID="$(id -g)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# User mapping prompt — simple yes/no, implementation details are hidden
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
read -rp "Map host user into container for workspace write access? [Y/n] " MAP_CHOICE
if [[ ! "${MAP_CHOICE:-Y}" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]]; then
  MAP_USER=1
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1Password token prompt
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]]; then
  echo ""
  log "1Password CLI requires a service account token"
  echo "Create one at: https://start.1password.com/settings/automation"
  read -rsp "Enter token: " ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY
  echo ""
  [[ -z "$ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY" ]] && error "Token required"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GitHub token prompt
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]]; then
  echo ""
  log "GitHub auth requires a fine-grained personal access token"
  echo "Create one at: https://github.com/settings/tokens?type=beta"
  echo "Recommended scopes: Contents (read/write), Metadata (read)"
  read -rsp "Enter GitHub token: " GH_TOKEN_VALUE
  echo ""
  [[ -z "$GH_TOKEN_VALUE" ]] && error "GitHub token required"

  DEFAULT_GH_NAME="$(git config --global user.name 2>/dev/null || true)"
  DEFAULT_GH_EMAIL="$(git config --global user.email 2>/dev/null || true)"

  read -rp "Git user.name [${DEFAULT_GH_NAME:-}]: " GH_USER_NAME
  GH_USER_NAME="${GH_USER_NAME:-$DEFAULT_GH_NAME}"
  [[ -z "$GH_USER_NAME" ]] && error "Git user.name required"

  read -rp "Git user.email [${DEFAULT_GH_EMAIL:-}]: " GH_USER_EMAIL
  GH_USER_EMAIL="${GH_USER_EMAIL:-$DEFAULT_GH_EMAIL}"
  [[ -z "$GH_USER_EMAIL" ]] && error "Git user.email required"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Dry-run summary
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "1" ]]; then
  echo ""
  echo "========================================"
  echo "DRY RUN — nothing will be created"
  echo "========================================"
  echo "Container:   $CONTAINER_NAME"
  echo "Image:       $BASE_IMAGE"
  echo "User:        $HOST_USER (UID:$HOST_UID GID:$HOST_GID)"
  echo "Workspace:   $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"
  echo "Write access: $( [[ "$MAP_USER" == "1" ]] && echo "yes (auto-detect method)" || echo "no" )"
  echo "Chromium:    yes"
  echo "Dev tools:   Oh My Zsh, fzf, bat"
  echo "Docker:      yes"
  echo "Proxy:       $( [[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]] && echo "${PROXY_IP:-auto}:$PROXY_PORT" || echo no )"
  echo "1Password:   $( [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]] && echo yes || echo no )"
  echo "GitHub auth: $( [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]] && echo "yes ($GH_USER_NAME <$GH_USER_EMAIL>)" || echo no )"
  echo "========================================"
  echo ""
  echo "Would run:"
  echo "  incus launch $BASE_IMAGE $CONTAINER_NAME"
  echo "  Enable container nesting for Docker"
  echo "  Install packages ($(echo "$BASE_IMAGE" | grep -q alpine && echo apk || echo apt))"
  echo "  Create user $HOST_USER inside container"
  echo "  Mount $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"
  [[ "$MAP_USER" == "1" ]] && echo "  Map UID/GID (try shift, fallback to raw.idmap)"
  echo "  Install: mise, Claude Code"
  echo "  Install Chromium (headless)"
  echo "  Install: Oh My Zsh, fzf, bat, shell aliases"
  echo "  Install Docker"
  [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]] && echo "  Install 1Password CLI (token via incus config env var)"
  [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]] && echo "  Install gh CLI, configure git auth (token via incus config env var)"
  [[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]] && echo "  Configure proxy"
  exit 0
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# From here on, real work — set up cleanup trap
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
trap cleanup_on_failure ERR

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Create container
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Creating container: $CONTAINER_NAME ($(basename "$BASE_IMAGE"))"
incus launch "$BASE_IMAGE" "$CONTAINER_NAME"

log "Enabling container nesting for Docker..."
incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" security.nesting=true
incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" security.syscalls.intercept.mknod=true
incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" security.syscalls.intercept.setxattr=true

# Docker-in-Incus hits two AppArmor problems:
#   1. runc fails with "ip_unprivileged_port_start: permission denied"
#      unless the container runs with an unconfined AppArmor profile.
#      (CVE-2025-52881, https://github.com/lxc/incus/pull/2624)
#   2. Even with unconfined, Docker reads /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled,
#      sees "Y", and tries to load the "docker-default" profile via securityfs —
#      which isn't accessible inside the container.
# Fix: set unconfined at the LXC level (fixes #1), then mask the kernel
# AppArmor "enabled" flag inside the container so Docker skips AppArmor
# entirely (fixes #2).  The Incus container itself remains the security
# boundary.
incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" raw.lxc "lxc.apparmor.profile=unconfined"
log "AppArmor set to unconfined inside container (required for Docker)."
# raw.lxc is only read at container start, so restart to apply it.
incus restart "$CONTAINER_NAME"

wait_for_container "$CONTAINER_NAME"
wait_for_network "$CONTAINER_NAME"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Install packages
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Installing packages..."
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -es <<PACKAGES
  if command -v apk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Ensure community repo is enabled (some Incus images only ship main)
    if ! grep -q '/community' /etc/apk/repositories 2>/dev/null; then
      MAIN_REPO=\$(grep '/main' /etc/apk/repositories | head -1)
      echo "\${MAIN_REPO%/main}/community" >> /etc/apk/repositories
    fi
    apk update && apk add --no-cache $APK_CORE $APK_BUILD $APK_DEVLIBS $APK_RUNTIME
  elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
    apt-get update -y
    apt-get install -y $APT_CORE $APT_BUILD $APT_DEVLIBS $APT_RUNTIME
    apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


    # Extra repos (mise + GitHub CLI)
    install -dm 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
    curl -fsSL https://mise.jdx.dev/gpg-key.pub | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/mise-archive-keyring.gpg
    echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/mise-archive-keyring.gpg] https://mise.jdx.dev/deb stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mise.list
    curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg 2>/dev/null
    chmod go+r /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
    echo "deb [arch=\$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list
    apt-get update -y
    apt-get install -y mise gh

  else
    echo 'Unsupported package manager' >&2; exit 1
  fi
PACKAGES

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Create user
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Creating user $HOST_USER..."
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "
  ZSH_PATH=\$(command -v zsh || echo /bin/bash)

  # Remove conflicting UID/GID owners
  EXISTING_USER=\$(getent passwd $HOST_UID | cut -d: -f1 || true)
  EXISTING_GROUP=\$(getent group $HOST_GID | cut -d: -f1 || true)

  if [ -n \"\$EXISTING_USER\" ] && [ \"\$EXISTING_USER\" != \"$HOST_USER\" ]; then
    userdel -r \"\$EXISTING_USER\" 2>/dev/null || userdel \"\$EXISTING_USER\" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  if [ -n \"\$EXISTING_GROUP\" ] && [ \"\$EXISTING_GROUP\" != \"$HOST_USER\" ]; then
    groupdel \"\$EXISTING_GROUP\" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi

  # Create group + user
  if command -v useradd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    groupadd -g $HOST_GID $HOST_USER 2>/dev/null || true
    useradd -m -u $HOST_UID -g $HOST_GID -s \"\$ZSH_PATH\" $HOST_USER || exit 1
  else
    addgroup -g $HOST_GID $HOST_USER 2>/dev/null || true
    adduser -D -u $HOST_UID -G $HOST_USER -s \"\$ZSH_PATH\" $HOST_USER || exit 1
  fi

  echo '$HOST_USER ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL' > /etc/sudoers.d/$HOST_USER
  chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/$HOST_USER

  id $HOST_USER >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'User creation failed' >&2; exit 1; }
"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Mount workspace
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Mounting workspace: $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"

if [[ "$MAP_USER" == "1" ]]; then
  mount_workspace "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$WORKSPACE_PATH" "$MOUNT_PATH" "$HOST_UID" "$HOST_GID"
else
  incus config device add "$CONTAINER_NAME" workspace disk \
    source="$WORKSPACE_PATH" path="$MOUNT_PATH"
  IDMAP_METHOD="none"
fi

# Final readiness check
wait_for_container "$CONTAINER_NAME"
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- id "$HOST_USER" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
  || error "User $HOST_USER does not exist. User creation may have failed."

wait_for_network "$CONTAINER_NAME"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shell setup — auto-cd and dev tools
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Configuring zsh..."
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'ZSHEOF'
  set -e

  # Auto-cd to /workspace on login
  touch ~/.zshrc
  cat >> ~/.zprofile <<'WORKSPACE'
if [[ -d /workspace ]]; then
    cd /workspace
fi
WORKSPACE

  # Activate mise in zsh
  echo 'eval "$(mise activate zsh)"' >> ~/.zshrc
ZSHEOF

log "Installing Oh My Zsh..."
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'ZSHEOF'
  set -e
  sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)" "" --unattended

  sed -i 's/^ZSH_THEME=".*"/ZSH_THEME="dpoggi"/' ~/.zshrc

  # Add [incus] prefix to prompt
  cat >> ~/.zshrc <<'RCBLOCK'

# [incus] prompt prefix
PROMPT="%{$fg[cyan]%}[incus]%{$reset_color%} ${PROMPT%»*}%{$reset_color%}"$'\n'"» "
RCBLOCK

  # Plugins
  git clone https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions \
    ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-~/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions
  sed -i 's/^plugins=(git)/plugins=(git zsh-autosuggestions)/' ~/.zshrc
ZSHEOF

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Docker
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Installing Docker..."

# Mask AppArmor so Docker doesn't try to use it (see AppArmor comment above).
# Bind-mount a file containing "N" over the kernel's AppArmor enabled flag.
# Docker's Go apparmor library reads this to decide whether to use AppArmor;
# returning "N" makes it skip AppArmor entirely.
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c 'bash -s' <<'APPARMOR_EOF'
    if [ -f /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled ] && \
       grep -q Y /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled 2>/dev/null; then
      echo N > /run/apparmor_disabled
      mount --bind /run/apparmor_disabled /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled

      # Make the mask persistent across reboots.
      if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        cat > /etc/systemd/system/mask-apparmor.service <<'UNIT'
[Unit]
Description=Mask AppArmor enabled flag for Docker-in-Incus
DefaultDependencies=no
Before=docker.service containerd.service docker.socket

[Service]
Type=oneshot
RemainAfterExit=yes
ExecStart=/bin/sh -c 'echo N > /run/apparmor_disabled && mount --bind /run/apparmor_disabled /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled'

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT
        systemctl daemon-reload
        systemctl enable mask-apparmor.service
      elif command -v rc-update >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # Alpine/OpenRC: create a proper init script with dependency ordering
        cat > /etc/init.d/mask-apparmor <<'OPENRC'
#!/sbin/openrc-run
description="Mask AppArmor enabled flag for Docker-in-Incus"

depend() {
    before docker containerd
    keyword -stop
}

start() {
    echo N > /run/apparmor_disabled
    mount --bind /run/apparmor_disabled /sys/module/apparmor/parameters/enabled
}
OPENRC
        chmod +x /etc/init.d/mask-apparmor
        rc-update add mask-apparmor boot
      fi
    fi
APPARMOR_EOF

incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c 'bash -s' <<'DOCKER_EOF'
    if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
      apt-get update && apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupg
      install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
      curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
      chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
      echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list
      apt-get update && apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin
      systemctl enable docker && systemctl start docker
    else
      apk add --no-cache docker docker-cli-compose
      rc-update add docker boot && service docker start
    fi
DOCKER_EOF
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "usermod -aG docker $HOST_USER 2>/dev/null || addgroup $HOST_USER docker 2>/dev/null || true"
wait_for_container "$CONTAINER_NAME"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Chromium
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Installing Playwright + Chromium dependencies..."
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c 'bash -s' <<'CHROMIUM_EOF'
    if command -v apk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      apk add --no-cache nss freetype harfbuzz ca-certificates ttf-freefont \
        mesa-gbm libxcomposite libxdamage libxrandr libxshmfence at-spi2-core \
        cups-libs libdrm pango alsa-lib dbus-libs
    elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
      # Playwright's bundled Chromium needs.
      apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
        libnss3 libnspr4 libatk1.0-0t64 libatk-bridge2.0-0t64 \
        libcups2t64 libdrm2 libxcomposite1 libxdamage1 libxfixes3 \
        libxrandr2 libgbm1 libpango-1.0-0 libcairo2 libasound2t64 \
        libatspi2.0-0t64 libxshmfence1 fonts-liberation xdg-utils
    fi
CHROMIUM_EOF

log "Installing Playwright + Chromium browser..."
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- npm install -g playwright
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'npx -y playwright install chromium'

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# fzf + bat (interactive search)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Installing fzf and bat via mise..."
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'EOF'
  set -e
  export MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS="/workspace"
  mise use -g go@latest fzf@latest bat@latest

  cat >> ~/.zshrc <<'FZF'

# fzf + bat aliases
alias f="fzf --preview 'bat {-1} --color=always'"
gdiff() {
  local preview="git diff $@ --color=always -- {-1}"
  git diff $@ --name-only | fzf -m --ansi --preview $preview
}
FZF
EOF

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entire CLI (optional)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$USE_ENTIRE" == "1" ]]; then
  log "Installing Entire CLI..."
  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'EOF'
    set -e
    eval "$(mise activate bash)"
    go install github.com/entireio/cli@latest
    entire enable
EOF
fi

# Run mise install if the workspace has a config
if [[ -f "$WORKSPACE_PATH/mise.toml" || -f "$WORKSPACE_PATH/.mise.toml" || -f "$WORKSPACE_PATH/.tool-versions" ]]; then
  log "Running mise install for workspace..."
  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'EOF'
    export MISE_TRUSTED_CONFIG_PATHS=/workspace;
    export MISE_NODE_COMPILE=0;
    cd $MOUNT_PATH && mise install
EOF
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1Password CLI (optional)
#
# The service account token is stored as a container-level environment
# variable via `incus config set`. This avoids writing it to disk or
# shell history inside the container. It is visible to anyone who can
# run `incus config show <container>` on the host.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]]; then
  log "Installing 1Password CLI..."
  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c '
    wget -q https://cache.agilebits.com/dist/1P/op2/pkg/v2.30.0/op_linux_amd64_v2.30.0.zip -O /tmp/op.zip
    unzip -q /tmp/op.zip -d /tmp/ && mv /tmp/op /usr/local/bin/ && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/op && rm /tmp/op.zip
  '
  log "Setting 1Password token via container environment..."
  incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" environment.OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="$ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY"
  unset ONEPASSWORD_SERVICE_KEY
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GitHub auth (optional)
#
# Injects the token as a container env var and configures git's credential
# helper via `gh auth setup-git`. gh CLI is already installed as a base
# package. Token never written to disk; unset from host shell after injection.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]]; then
  log "Setting GitHub token via container environment..."
  incus config set "$CONTAINER_NAME" environment.GH_TOKEN="$GH_TOKEN_VALUE"

  log "Configuring git credential helper and identity..."
  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" --env "GH_TOKEN=$GH_TOKEN_VALUE" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c "
    gh auth setup-git
    git config --global user.name '$GH_USER_NAME'
    git config --global user.email '$GH_USER_EMAIL'
  "
  unset GH_TOKEN_VALUE
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Claude Code
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- su - "$HOST_USER" -c 'bash -s' <<'CLAUDE'
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
  echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
CLAUDE
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Proxy (optional)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]]; then
  [[ -z "$PROXY_IP" ]] && PROXY_IP="$(incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "ip route | awk '/default/ {print \$3}'")"
  log "Configuring proxy: $PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT"
  incus exec "$CONTAINER_NAME" -- sh -c "cat >/etc/profile.d/claude-proxy.sh <<PROXYEOF
export HTTP_PROXY='http://$PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT'
export HTTPS_PROXY='http://$PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT'
export NO_PROXY='localhost,127.0.0.1'
PROXYEOF
chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/claude-proxy.sh"
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Disable cleanup trap on success
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
trap - ERR

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Done
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Container ready!"
echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "Container:   $CONTAINER_NAME"
echo "Image:       $BASE_IMAGE"
echo "User:        $HOST_USER (UID:$HOST_UID GID:$HOST_GID)"
echo "Workspace:   $WORKSPACE_PATH -> $MOUNT_PATH"
echo "Write access: ${IDMAP_METHOD:-none}"
echo "Chromium:    installed"
echo "Dev tools:   Oh My Zsh, fzf, bat"
echo "Docker:      installed"
[[ "$USE_PROXY" == "1" ]]     && echo "Proxy:       http://$PROXY_IP:$PROXY_PORT"
[[ "$USE_1PASSWORD" == "1" ]] && echo "1Password:   installed (token in container env)"
[[ "$USE_GH_TOKEN" == "1" ]] && echo "GitHub auth: configured ($GH_USER_NAME <$GH_USER_EMAIL>)"
echo "========================================"
echo ""
echo "Shell in:"
echo "  incus exec $CONTAINER_NAME -- su - $HOST_USER"
echo ""
echo "Start Claude Code:"
echo "  incus exec $CONTAINER_NAME -- su - $HOST_USER -c 'cd $MOUNT_PATH && claude'"

content.text:
Two issues I see:

1. **Line 73-77**: `source "$SCRIPT_DIR/incus.macos.setup"` runs *before* args are parsed (line 266), so `COLIMA_CPUS`/`COLIMA_MEMORY`/`COLIMA_DISK` from `--colima-*` flags won't be set yet. The macOS setup will always use defaults.

2. **Line 288-292**: There's a *second* `source "$SCRIPT_DIR/incus.macos.setup"` block after arg parsing — but that's the one we added. The first one at line 73 is leftover.
Yep, duplicated. The first one (line 73) runs before args are parsed so the colima flags are ignored. Need to remove it and keep only the one after arg parsing.
